lessons: When a multi-page document contains conflicting reference numbers in its headers, the extraction should either capture all variations or a note should be made about the inconsistency.
For service dates presented as a range (e.g., 'MM/DD-MM/DD/YYYY'), the full range should be extracted to avoid data truncation.
When a claim-level provider name is not explicitly stated, the document-level payee name should be used instead of the Provider NPI.
Do not use information from previous documents or external context to populate fields like 'payer'. The field should only contain information explicitly present in the current source document.
Carefully distinguish between different numerical identifiers on the page. In this case, '1076' was a document reference number, while 'J076' was part of the page numbering information.
Ensure that columns like 'Contractual Obligation' on Aetna EOBs are correctly mapped to 'provider_discount', even when the value is zero.
The 'Policy #' field on Heartland National documents should be mapped to 'subscriber_id'.
Claim-level remark codes, such as those found in the header row on AVESIS documents, must be captured. If no claim-level field exists, they should be associated with the first service line.
Be cautious with concatenating multiple identifiers into a single `document_reference` field; extract the primary reference number.

The provided SCHEMA RULES did not match the structure of any of the three source document types (Aetna, Heartland, AVESIS). Applying a schema for one payer format to another's document can lead to mapping errors, such as incorrectly assigning a negative sign to the 'Prov Adj' value from the AVESIS document or misinterpreting column names.

The AVESIS document format contains multiple identifiers in the 'Plan' column. The extraction logic should be enhanced to capture all of these values, correctly associating them with fields like 'group_id' and 'patient_acct_number'.
Service-line level remark codes, which can appear in various columns on the AVESIS document (e.g., the 'Insured ID' column), must be consistently captured in the `explanation_codes` field.

When multiple documents are present in a batch, ensure that the structure for common objects like 'virtual_card' is consistent across all document metadata entries. In this case, 'payment_id' should be consistently nested within the 'virtual_card' object.

When a source document column header is semantically equivalent but not identical to a schema field (e.g., 'Excluded Charges' vs. 'provider_discount'), the data should be mapped to the appropriate schema field to prevent critical financial data loss.
If a document format lacks a 'Units' column, each service line should be inferred as 1 unit unless there is explicit information to the contrary.
